ocr_mistral.text:
War of 1861. Louisville
CLAIM FOR WIDOW'S PENSION. Act of July 14, 1862.
105072
Brief in the case of Rose Chambers, Widow of
Aaron Chambers, Private Co G, 118th Regt. U.S.C. Troops.
resident of Henderson County and State of Ky.
Post Office address: Henderson, Ky.

DECLARATION AND IDENTIFICATION IN DUE FORM.
PROOF EXHIBITED.

Service. The soldier was enrolled Sept. 19, 1864, and is reported by
the Adjutant General.
"Died November 6, 1864, of disease in field Hospital Va."

Death. Testimony of the 1st Sergeant of same Co. and Regt that the soldier died
date as above of Diarrhea contracted in the service, and that he was a
man of sound physical body when he entered the Army, a good soldier and
of steady and sober habits. Two other persons testify to his sound condition at
enlistment. Affidavit of Claimant that the officers of the Regt being northern
men she is unable to procure a commissioned officer certificate etc.

Marriage. Proof of Cohabitation since 1837 and recognition as man and wife by the
community since that time and up to the time when he enlisted. No legal
marriage the State law requiring none for persons of Color.

Names and dates of birth of children. None under 16 years now living.

Loyalty. Shown

Agent and his P.O. address. None address Claimant Henderson, Ky.

Admitted December 13, 1867, to a Pension of $ Eight per month,
commencing November 7, 1864.
P.H. Berkaw, Examining Clerk.

Appd. S.F.S.

transcribe_handwriting.text:
Louisville

Act of July 14, 1862

Claim for Widow's Pension.


Brief in the case of 
Aree Chambers
Widow of
Newton Chambers   
Pvt Co. H. 8th Regt W. C. Troops
resident of Henderson County and State of Ky
Post Office address: Henderson Ky.

Declaration and Identification in due form.

Proof Exhibited.

Service:

The soldier was mustered Sept. 19, 1865, and is reported by

his regiment commander

"Died November 16, 1864 of disease in field Hospital Va."


Death:

Testimony of the 1st Sergeant of same Co. and Reg't that the soldier died 

Date as above, of disease contracted in the service, and that he was a

man of sound & physical body when he entered the army, a good soldier and

of steady and sober habits, in other years truly to be cared [unclear] in

performance of all soldiers. In support of the proof of the death, surgeon mentions

name tho as unable to procure a commissioned officer, certifies to it.

Marriage:

Proof of cohabitation since 1837 and recognition as man and wife by the

community since that time and up to the time when he enlisted. No legal 

marriage then state law requiring some proof previous to 1900.

Names and dates 
of birth of children.

None under 16 years remaining

Loyalty:

Shown

Agent

Respondent: None address. Claimant Henderson Ky

Admitted December 13, 1867 to a Pension of $ Eight per month,

commencing November 7, 1864.

Appd.

S.G.S. P.H. Perham

Examining Clerk.

marriage then state law requiring some proof previous to 1900.
Names and dates of birth of children.
Respondent: None address. Claimant Henderson Ky
Admitted December 13, 1867 to a Pension of $ Eight per month,
